summary:
The incision described—within the hairline and angling at 45 degrees toward the zygoma—is characteristic of the Gillies temporal incision used for accessing zygomatic fractures. It provides optimal cosmetic results by blending into natural hairline contours during facial reconstruction or fracture repair procedures involving the midface region.
